entries.title: Run self-verify after modifying UI surfaces
entries.message:
You modified a UI / harness surface. Unit tests cover function-level logic but NOT the rendered lifecycle (modal mount/unmount, slash-menu navigation, askcard flow, focus chain, toast levels). Before claiming done, run:

  bun run src/index.ts self-verify --agentic \
    --goal "<describe the user-visible flow you just modified>" \
    --llm "deepseek-v4-flash" --turns 8

This catches integration bugs no vitest test can see — for example the 2026-05-21 session where typing /agents opened the slash-menu instead of the subagents-modal directly. The harness reports the actual semantic state so the LLM observes the divergence and flags it.

Cost: ~$0.005-0.01 per run, ~30s wall-clock. Pre-push hook auto-runs Tier 1 (heuristic) on these surfaces.
entries.why: 2026-05-21 incident: composer/modal/slash-menu interactions caused user-visible regressions that passed all unit tests because the harness lifecycle (focus restore, isModal flag, askcard children) is not reachable from jest/vitest renders. Tier 2 (LLM-in-the-loop) found a goal-vs-reality mismatch that no scripted test could express.
entries.how_to_apply: When the warning fires: pause before the next Edit. Either (a) run self-verify with a goal narrating the user flow you just changed, OR (b) acknowledge that the change is internal-only (e.g., refactor with no UI behavior change) and proceed. Do NOT skip silently if the change touches user-visible surfaces.

evidence.summary: DeepSeek-v4-flash drove inner CLI, detected slash-menu id=slash-menu opened instead of expected id=subagents-modal after typing /agents + Enter. verdict=fail with actionable reason.

entries.title: Prefer LSP impact queries before grep
entries.message:
Before running a broad grep/ripgrep for a symbol, prefer the LSP impact tools — `impact_of_change`, `lsp_query` (findReferences/hover), `wait_for_diagnostics`. They return structured references + diagnostics + rename safety instead of raw text hits.

Grep-fallback rule: call `wait_for_diagnostics` or `impact_of_change` first and read `lspStatus`:
- `lspStatus === 'ok'` → use the LSP results; do NOT grep-as-fallback.
- `lspStatus !== 'ok'` ('partial' or 'unavailable') → grep fallback is allowed; the LSP was not fully available.

Never decide grep-safety from `diagnostics.length`. `lspStatus` is the single source of truth, computed by the manager from timeout/publish state (SLICE1-BUILD-NOTE.md §73-86).
entries.why: Slice 1 of the semantic workbench adds composite LSP impact analysis so in-runtime agents work semantically instead of by blind text search. Grepping a symbol on a non-ready buffer yields false confidence (missed structural refs, stale results); the lspStatus gate makes the fallback decision explicit and testable (self-verify harness src/lsp/__tests__/slice1-self-verify.test.ts).
entries.how_to_apply: When about to grep for a symbol/definition/references: first call impact_of_change (or wait_for_diagnostics) on the file. If lspStatus is 'ok', trust the structured result and skip grep. If lspStatus is 'partial'/'unavailable', fall back to grep. For pure text/string searches unrelated to code symbols, grep directly.

evidence.summary: Authoritative Slice 1 contract: lspStatus 'ok'|'partial'|'unavailable'; grep fallback allowed only when lspStatus !== 'ok'.

ingestion.notes: Run from muonroi-cli root. Auth via EE_AUTH_TOKEN env or ~/.experience/config.json:serverAuthToken. Ingests into experience-behavioral collection (muonroi-cli-behavioral is not in EE's KNOWN_COLLECTIONS yet; scope_project_slug=muonroi-cli narrows it correctly). Payload contains both the nested experience-core schema (payload.json with scope+solution+confidence) AND flat scope_* keys required by the Qdrant index-level pre-filter. Verify with `curl /api/search`. Surfacing via /api/intercept also depends on EE's brain-relevance LLM gate at runtime — direct search proves the entry exists; intercept surfacing varies with edit semantics. Local PostToolUse hook in .claude/hooks/self-qa-post-edit.cjs fires independently of EE.
